steps.title: Find the defining principle
steps.content: The decisive signal is the passage's final statement: “none of these factors in isolation fully explains pedestrian habits in a given city.” This tells us the author views pedestrian amenities as one of many necessary factors, not a sufficient cause by themselves.

steps.title: Match the principle to the choices
steps.content:
The author's central point is that no single factor—including amenities—fully explains pedestrian behavior; many factors work together.

Choice D directly captures this: amenities are “just one of several factors.” The other choices misread the passage (A claims amenities harm other cities, B inverts cause-versus-effect without textual basis, C rank-orders factors without evidence).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
